customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety. Chris was cooperative, low-friction, and proactively offered scheduling flexibility. No skepticism signals, no objections raised. Primary risk is structural (spouse exclusion), not attitudinal.
unresolved_concerns: Wife is on the title and was not included in the appointment. She is a co-decision-maker whose absence makes the sit structurally vulnerable. Closer should confirm at the top of the call whether the wife will be present.

Bill-contingent booking: rep stated 'I can schedule you right after you send that.' Bill was apparently received on-call (rep confirmed at 05:34), but closer should verify the bill is on file before the appointment.
Customer's motivation for filling out the solar form was never surfaced (interest_in_solar verify item skipped). Closer has no emotional anchor language from the setter call to build on.
No prior_designs question was asked — competitive context is unknown. Closer should probe early whether Chris has seen other proposals.
exact_promises_rep_made: Text reminder will be sent after the call.
No upfront cost for panels and installation.
If solar cannot save money in the first year, Ion is not allowed to offer anything.
closer_should_know: The wife is on the title and was not included in the appointment. Before running the proposal, confirm she is on the call — if she is not, the closer should pause and reschedule rather than present to Chris alone. The setter never asked what interested Chris in solar, so the closer is walking in without an emotional anchor from the setter conversation.
